The Workmen’s Compensation Act requires employers to notify the Social Security Office (SSO) of any employee’s injury, sickness, disappearance, or death due to work. In addition, the Occupational Safety, Health and Environment Act requires employers to immediately report any work-related fatality to a designated government occupational safety and health (OSH) inspector.
Please enter the number of work related fatalities for the given year.

Please enter the total number of major injuries for the given year that have caused an employee to lose a body part, suffer permanent disability, or be unable to work for more than three consecutive days.
Do not include injuries already reported above.
Please enter the number of other injuries for the given year.

Please provide the number of employees reported as infected or suspected of having an occupational disease to the Public Health Office, according to the R506 reporting system.\n\nOccupational and Environmental Diseases Control Act, B.E. 2562
Please enter the number of cases of occupational diseases/illnesses for the given year:

Please provide the number of reportable dangerous occurrences (including serious near-miss events) as required to be notified to the authorities under the Occupational Safety, Health, and Work Environment Act, B.E. 2554.\n\nOccupational Safety, Health and Environment Act B.E. 2554, Section 34
Please enter the number of dangerous occurrences (or near misses) for the given year.

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.
